media.base_medium: Widdel & Bak (1992) marine defined sulfate-reducer medium, as reproduced in the Laso-Perez et al. 2018 Nat. Protoc. hydrocarbon-enrichment protocol (cited as SRM, ref 65). Carbon/electron source replaced by petroleum n-alkanes; sulfate as electron acceptor.

components.name: Petroleum n-alkanes (C5-C14; e.g. n-pentane, n-hexane ... n-tetradecane)
components.concentration: 200 ul liquid alkane per 100 ml culture
components.evidence: Cultures were supplemented with 200 ul liquid alkane (C5-C14) in duplicates ... amended with petroleum alkanes (C5-C14) as sole carbon and electron source and sulfate as electron acceptor

components.name: 2,2,4,4,6,8,8-Heptamethylnonane (HMN)
components.evidence: For the C5-C10 alkanes, 4 ml 2,2,4,4,6,8,8-heptamethylnonane (HMN) were added to mitigate potential toxic effects of the substrate

components.name: Sodium sulfate (Na2SO4)
components.evidence: The basal medium per liter contained 24.32 g NaCl, 10.0 g MgCl2*6H2O, 1.5 g CaCl2*2H2O, 4.0 g Na2SO4, 0.66 g KCl, and 0.09 g KBr

components.name: Sodium chloride (NaCl)
components.evidence: The basal medium per liter contained 24.32 g NaCl, 10.0 g MgCl2*6H2O, 1.5 g CaCl2*2H2O, 4.0 g Na2SO4, 0.66 g KCl, and 0.09 g KBr

components.name: Magnesium chloride hexahydrate (MgCl2*6H2O)
components.evidence: The basal medium per liter contained 24.32 g NaCl, 10.0 g MgCl2*6H2O, 1.5 g CaCl2*2H2O, 4.0 g Na2SO4, 0.66 g KCl, and 0.09 g KBr

components.name: Calcium chloride dihydrate (CaCl2*2H2O)
components.evidence: The basal medium per liter contained 24.32 g NaCl, 10.0 g MgCl2*6H2O, 1.5 g CaCl2*2H2O, 4.0 g Na2SO4, 0.66 g KCl, and 0.09 g KBr

components.name: Potassium chloride (KCl)
components.evidence: The basal medium per liter contained 24.32 g NaCl, 10.0 g MgCl2*6H2O, 1.5 g CaCl2*2H2O, 4.0 g Na2SO4, 0.66 g KCl, and 0.09 g KBr

components.name: Potassium bromide (KBr)
components.evidence: The basal medium per liter contained 24.32 g NaCl, 10.0 g MgCl2*6H2O, 1.5 g CaCl2*2H2O, 4.0 g Na2SO4, 0.66 g KCl, and 0.09 g KBr

components.name: Ammonium chloride (NH4Cl)
components.evidence: supplemented with: NH4Cl (2 mM), KH2PO4 (1 mM), CO2-saturated sodium bicarbonate (30 mM)

components.name: Monopotassium phosphate (KH2PO4)
components.evidence: supplemented with: NH4Cl (2 mM), KH2PO4 (1 mM), CO2-saturated sodium bicarbonate (30 mM)

components.name: Sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO3)
components.evidence: supplemented with: ... CO2-saturated sodium bicarbonate (30 mM)

components.name: Sodium sulfide nonahydrate (Na2S*9H2O)
components.evidence: The anoxic medium was reduced by addition of Na2S (final concentration: ~1 mM) and few crystals of sodium dithionite

components.name: Sodium dithionite (Na2S2O4)
components.evidence: The anoxic medium was reduced by addition of Na2S (final concentration: ~1 mM) and few crystals of sodium dithionite

components.name: Resazurin (sodium salt)
components.concentration: 0.5 mg/L (0.5 ml of 0.1% w/v per L)
components.evidence: Sodium resazurin (0.1% w/v): 0.50 ml [per liter]

components.name: Trace element solution SL-10
components.evidence: supplemented with: ... 1 ml/l of trace element solution SL10, 0.2 ml/l of selenite-tungsten solution, and 2 ml/l of a solution of 10 vitamins

components.name: Iron(II) chloride tetrahydrate (FeCl2*4H2O)
components.concentration: 1.5 g/L in SL-10 (added 1 ml/L; ~1.5 mg/L final)
components.evidence: Trace Element Solution SL-10 (per 1000 ml): HCl (25%) 10 ml, FeCl2*4H2O 1.5 g, ZnCl2 70 mg, MnCl2*4H2O 100 mg, H3BO3 6 mg, CoCl2*6H2O 190 mg, CuCl2*2H2O 2 mg, NiCl2*6H2O 24 mg, Na2MoO4*2H2O 36 mg

components.name: Zinc chloride (ZnCl2)
components.evidence: Trace Element Solution SL-10 (per 1000 ml): ... ZnCl2 70 mg ...

components.name: Manganese(II) chloride tetrahydrate (MnCl2*4H2O)
components.evidence: Trace Element Solution SL-10 (per 1000 ml): ... MnCl2*4H2O 100 mg ...

components.name: Boric acid (H3BO3)
components.evidence: Trace Element Solution SL-10 (per 1000 ml): ... H3BO3 6 mg ...

components.name: Cobalt(II) chloride hexahydrate (CoCl2*6H2O)
components.evidence: Trace Element Solution SL-10 (per 1000 ml): ... CoCl2*6H2O 190 mg ...

components.name: Copper(II) chloride dihydrate (CuCl2*2H2O)
components.evidence: Trace Element Solution SL-10 (per 1000 ml): ... CuCl2*2H2O 2 mg ...

components.name: Nickel(II) chloride hexahydrate (NiCl2*6H2O)
components.evidence: Trace Element Solution SL-10 (per 1000 ml): ... NiCl2*6H2O 24 mg ...

components.name: Sodium molybdate dihydrate (Na2MoO4*2H2O)
components.evidence: Trace Element Solution SL-10 (per 1000 ml): ... Na2MoO4*2H2O 36 mg ...

components.name: Selenite-tungstate solution
components.evidence: supplemented with: ... 0.2 ml/l of selenite-tungsten solution ...

components.name: Sodium selenite pentahydrate (Na2SeO3*5H2O)
components.evidence: Selenite-Tungstate Solution (per 1000 ml): NaOH 0.5 g, Na2SeO3*5H2O 3 mg, Na2WO4*2H2O 4 mg

components.name: Sodium tungstate dihydrate (Na2WO4*2H2O)
components.evidence: Selenite-Tungstate Solution (per 1000 ml): NaOH 0.5 g, Na2SeO3*5H2O 3 mg, Na2WO4*2H2O 4 mg

components.name: Sodium hydroxide (NaOH)
components.concentration: 0.5 g/L in selenite-tungstate stock; also pH adjust to 7.2-7.5
components.evidence: Selenite-Tungstate Solution (per 1000 ml): NaOH 0.5 g ... ; The pH was adjusted to 7.2-7.5 with 4 M NaOH

components.name: Vitamin solution (10 vitamins)
components.evidence: supplemented with: ... and 2 ml/l of a solution of 10 vitamins

components.name: Biotin
components.evidence: Wolin's Vitamin Solution 10x (per 1000 ml): Biotin 20 mg, Folic acid 20 mg, Pyridoxine HCl 100 mg, Thiamine HCl 50 mg, Riboflavin 50 mg, Nicotinic acid 50 mg, Calcium D-(+)-pantothenate 50 mg, Vitamin B12 1 mg, p-Aminobenzoic acid 50 mg, (DL)-alpha-Lipoic acid 50 mg

components.name: Folic acid
components.evidence: Wolin's Vitamin Solution 10x (per 1000 ml): ... Folic acid 20 mg ...

components.name: Pyridoxine HCl
components.evidence: Wolin's Vitamin Solution 10x (per 1000 ml): ... Pyridoxine HCl 100 mg ...

components.name: Thiamine HCl
components.evidence: Wolin's Vitamin Solution 10x (per 1000 ml): ... Thiamine HCl 50 mg ...

components.name: Riboflavin
components.evidence: Wolin's Vitamin Solution 10x (per 1000 ml): ... Riboflavin 50 mg ...

components.name: Nicotinic acid (niacin)
components.evidence: Wolin's Vitamin Solution 10x (per 1000 ml): ... Nicotinic acid 50 mg ...

components.name: Calcium D-(+)-pantothenate
components.evidence: Wolin's Vitamin Solution 10x (per 1000 ml): ... Calcium D-(+)-pantothenate 50 mg ...

components.name: Vitamin B12 (cyanocobalamin)
components.evidence: Wolin's Vitamin Solution 10x (per 1000 ml): ... Vitamin B12 1 mg ...

components.name: p-Aminobenzoic acid (PABA)
components.evidence: Wolin's Vitamin Solution 10x (per 1000 ml): ... p-Aminobenzoic acid 50 mg ...

components.name: (DL)-alpha-Lipoic acid (thioctic acid)
components.evidence: Wolin's Vitamin Solution 10x (per 1000 ml): ... (DL)-alpha-Lipoic acid 50 mg ...

components.name: N2:CO2 gas (90:10)
components.evidence: Headspaces were filled with N2:CO2 (90:10; 1 atm overpressure) and incubated at 70 C

components.name: Water (H2O)
components.evidence: The basal medium per liter contained 24.32 g NaCl ... [aqueous marine medium]

sources_chased.what: base recipe cited as ref 65 (Laso-Perez et al. 2018, Nat. Protoc. 13:1310-1330, doi 10.1038/nprot.2018.030) 'synthetic sulfate-reducer medium (SRM)'
sources_chased.how: WebFetch nature.com/experiments.springernature.com both redirect to IDP login (paywalled); recipe recovered instead from its own base, Widdel & Bak 1992

sources_chased.what: Widdel & Bak (1992) marine defined sulfate-reducer medium (the base that Laso-Perez 2018 SRM reproduces)
sources_chased.how: WebSearch returning a verbatim reproduction of the Widdel & Bak marine medium (Gram-negative mesophilic sulfate-reducing bacteria, The Prokaryotes)

sources_chased.what: DSMZ MediaDive medium 383 (Widdel-type marine SR medium) to expand named stock solutions
PMC10322722 main text (medium application, substrate, gas phase)

missing_before: Petroleum n-alkanes C5-C14 (carbon/electron source was entirely absent)
Vitamins other than B12: biotin, folic acid, pyridoxine, thiamine, riboflavin, nicotinic acid, pantothenate, PABA, lipoic acid
media.notes: SRM = Widdel & Bak (1992) marine defined sulfate-reducer medium, applied as an anoxic sediment slurry (10% sediment / 90% SRM) with petroleum n-alkanes (C5-C14) as sole carbon/electron source and sulfate as electron acceptor, 70 C, N2:CO2 (90:10) headspace. The ref-65 Nat. Protoc. PDF is paywalled, so exact bulk-salt/buffer/reductant concentrations were taken from the Widdel & Bak marine medium it reproduces, and the named SL-10, selenite-tungstate and 10-vitamin stock solutions were expanded from DSMZ MediaDive 383 (same Widdel lineage). Trace-metal/vitamin values are stock-solution amounts; final medium concentrations are ~1000x lower (SL-10/vitamins added at 1-2 ml/L) and are left as null where not confidently convertible.
